potential_missing.source_sentence: This behavior allows the user to easily control playback, pause when needed, switch discs, and stop the music whenever they want.

potential_hallucinations.requirement: The system shall start in a stopped state.
The system shall stop the music temporarily when paused but allow resuming from the same point.
The system shall return to a stopped state when the player is stopped.
The system shall allow switching to another disc and continuing playback if multiple discs are available.
The system shall remain in a stopped state when no more discs are left.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: When paused, the music stops temporarily, but it can easily continue from the same point when play is pressed again.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall resume playing from the last paused position when play is pressed.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall start in a stopped state.
hallucinations.reason: The source text does not explicitly state that the CD player starts in a stopped state, only that it usually does so. This could be inferred but is not directly stated.

ambiguities.rewritten_requirement: The system shall allow switching to another disc and continuing playback if multiple discs are available.
ambiguities.reason: This requirement is somewhat ambiguous as it does not specify the behavior when a new disc is inserted or how the player handles the transition between discs.
